author: codex-root

patch.value:
## Public surface

- RelationshipKindRegistry and schema validation.
- Append, remove or supersede, and inspect relationship events.
- Snapshot-aware graph reader and index interfaces.
- Bounded adjacency, path, closure, impact, and subgraph queries.
- Explain and cost metadata suitable for token-aware context selection.
- Import, export, merge, and extension hooks built from the same contract.

## Non-goals

This item does not choose product-specific relevance weights, silently invent relationships, or force large indexes on scratch projects. Those belong to context-engine policy, audits, and adaptive storage layers.
SDK relationship registry and graph-query primitives: custom typed edges, adjacency, paths, closures, impact, and bounded subgraphs
Promote relationship definition, mutation, indexing, and bounded traversal into stable public SDK APIs so every adapter and domain model shares one implementation.

The registry defines built-in and extension-provided kinds with direction, inverse, order-bearing and hierarchy flags, cardinality, payload schema, lifecycle, aliases, and compatibility version; Mutation APIs validate endpoints and invariants then append attributable immutable events without direct state rewrites; Read APIs cover adjacency, predecessors, successors, ancestors, descendants, shortest and constrained paths, transitive closure, reverse impact, and induced bounded subgraphs; Every query supports deterministic ordering, typed filters, field projection, cursor or streaming continuation, cancellation, snapshot selection, and explicit cost or truncation metadata; Indexes are rebuildable from history, incrementally maintained, crash safe, observable, and optional for small projects; Thin CLI and MCP adapters call public SDK APIs without hidden core imports; shell-safe JSON, JSONL, TOON, and counts-first outputs preserve pipeline composability; Contract, property, replay, concurrency, migration, and scale tests cover legacy kinds, custom kinds, cycles, snapshots, and one million items
Make the public SDK the complete source of truth for relationship schemas and graph operations
CLI, MCP, extensions, scripts, and non-PM applications can build on one deterministic and extensible graph contract
Removes adapter-local graph logic and unlocks typed domain relationships, scalable context assembly, and future graph algorithms
Versioned SDK APIs for kind registries, immutable mutations, adjacency and path queries, closures, impact traversal, and bounded subgraphs
The CLI has dependency operations and visualization, but custom semantic kinds and complete public graph primitives lack a canonical SDK contract

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Duplicate check 2026-07-13: SDK-first inventory contains generic query and lifecycle promotion slices but no stable typed relationship registry plus bounded graph algorithm API; this is a new promotion slice under pm-96h7 and relates to pm-usfg.

author: codex-gpt5-root
message: Track test run summary (test-local-mrkckbng-g4g06q)

value.author: codex-gpt5-root
value.text: Evidence: delivered the first broad public graph foundation: versioned built-in/custom relationship registry, alias compatibility, immutable in-memory index construction from item metadata, deterministic adjacency/closure/reverse-impact/shortest-path/subgraph queries with cancellation and explicit cost/truncation metadata, SDK barrel/docs, and validate integration. Packed tarball consumer proved a custom company owns graph plus real CLI lifecycle. Full repository run passed 286 files/5664 tests; focused all-source coverage for relationships.ts is 100/100/100/100. Remaining event mutation, durable incremental index, snapshot/streaming, MCP projection, and million-item scale acceptance stay on this feature, so it will return to open rather than be falsely closed.

value.author: codex-gpt5-root
value.text: A storage-independent relationship registry is the semantic boundary: algorithms must consume ordering/hierarchy flags rather than infer meaning from labels, while durable event/index implementations can remain interchangeable.

author: codex-gpt5-root
message: Pause after completing the broad registry/query/validation foundation; retain remaining durable mutation/index/snapshot/streaming/MCP/scale acceptance as open follow-up on the same canonical feature.

message: Address exact-head PR #554 relationship graph and lifecycle review findings

value.author: codex-root
value.text: PR #554 review evidence: addressed all ten first-round findings across construction complexity/performance, bounded closure/path truncation, malformed/custom lifecycle dependencies, immutable payload schemas, canonical dependency conversion, inverse-kind filters, generated changelog accuracy, and duplicate-edge documentation. Focused relationship coverage is exact 100/100/100/100; full repository run passed 286 files and 5664 tests before the final single branch regression was added. The broader durable event/index acceptance remains open.

author: codex-root
message: Address PR #554 second-round indexed graph and registry validation findings

value.author: codex-root
value.text: PR #554 second-round evidence: deduplicated bidirectional candidate accounting, changed induced subgraph assembly from whole-graph O(E) filtering to bounded adjacency-index work, enforced alias identifiers, and restored the no-guessing contract for unregistered dependency kinds. Registered custom ordering remains supported by the SDK registry; CLI extension-registry injection belongs to the remaining adapter acceptance. Focused tests pass 95/95, relationship coverage is exact 100/100/100/100, ESLint and build pass.

Lifecycle graph construction accepts an injected RelationshipKindRegistry, with tests proving custom ordering kinds participate and custom associative kinds do not. Focused relationship coverage is exact 100/100/100/100; 95 focused tests, ESLint, and build pass.

Focused 95 tests pass, relationship coverage is exact 100/100/100/100, ESLint and build pass.

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: Optimistic append sequences plus cursor fingerprints bind every page and snapshot to a coherent immutable relationship history; durable adapters can persist the same event contract without coupling graph semantics to storage.

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: Implementation evidence: added explicit ordering precedence to the extensible relationship registry; immutable deterministic graph node enumeration; and an append-only attributable RelationshipEventLog with stable relationship identities, optimistic version checks, add/remove/supersede events, cardinality validation, immutable replay snapshots by version/time, and opaque cursor pagination. Public packed SDK consumer proof imported @unbrained/pm-cli/sdk in a fresh project and replayed a custom company owns relationship plus delivery dependencies successfully.

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: Remaining canonical scope after this PR: durable storage adapters, native CLI/MCP graph query integration, migration/backfill, and million-item storage validation; keep feature open.

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: PR #556 review round 1 evidence: accepted and resolved graph-kernel findings by caching the immutable sorted node snapshot, introducing explicit source_parent/target_parent hierarchy orientation, and documenting built-in versus custom semantics. Repeated nodes() identity and hierarchy validation regressions pass; full repository coverage is exact 100/100/100/100.

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: PR #556 review round 2 evidence: added the public indexed incidentEdges() graph primitive and changed relationship append normalization from whole-universe graph reconstruction to validated two-endpoint normalization. Malformed source and target inputs are covered; immutable incident snapshots are deterministic and frozen.

author: codex-gpt5
message: Address PR #556 indexed ledger and sequence-consistent snapshot review

value.author: codex-gpt5
value.text: Evidence for PR #556 final review tranche: replaced O(R) active-edge cardinality scans with maintained O(1) identity/outgoing-one/incoming-one indexes covering add, supersede, remove, and slot reuse; timestamp snapshots now replay the contiguous append-sequence prefix through the last qualifying timestamp even for non-monotonic event time. Regression tests cover non-monotonic replay, empty pre-history snapshots, active-count independence, and cardinality-slot reuse. Verification: 287/287 files and 5,683/5,683 tests passed with exact 100% statements/branches/functions/lines (36,684/26,338/7,410/35,813); lint, typecheck, jscpd zero clones, 100% docstring gate, package boundary, and token-budget gates passed. Fresh packed @unbrained/pm-cli consumer appended 10,000 relationships in 207 ms and correctly materialized a 10,002-event non-monotonic snapshot. Gemini threads PRRT_kwDORSgRDc6Qutwn and PRRT_kwDORSgRDc6Qutwt were reacted to, replied to, and resolved. Item remains open for later durable-adapter work; active implementation is complete for this tranche.
